title: Bridge Essay: Social-semantic knowledge in frontotemporal dementia and after anterior temporal

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through Social-semantic, knowledge, in, frontotemporal, dementia.

**Social-semantic knowledge in frontotemporal dementia and after anterior temporal lobe resection.** — Brain Commun. PMID: 39513090. DOI: 10.1093/braincomms/fcae378. Degraded semantic memory is aprominent feature of frontotemporal dementia (FTD). It is classically associated with semantic dementia and anterior temporal lobe (ATL) atrophy, but semantic knowledge can also be compromised in behavioural variant FTD. Motivated by understanding behavioural change in FTD, recent research has focused selectively on social-semantic knowledge, with proposals that the right ATL is specialized for social concepts.
